subject: Mathematics
q: The value of $\cos 255^{\circ}+\sin 195^{\circ}$ is
question: The value of $\cos 255^{\circ}+\sin 195^{\circ}$ is
solution:
Consider $\cos 255^{\circ}+\sin 195^{\circ}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\cos \left(270^{\circ}-15^{\circ}\right)+\sin \left(180^{\circ}+15^{\circ}\right) \\
& =-\sin 15^{\circ}-\sin 15^{\circ} \\
& =-2 \sin 15^{\circ}=-2\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)=-\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be such that $\pi < \alpha-\beta < 3 \pi$. If $\sin \alpha+\sin \beta=-\frac{21}{65}$ and $\cos \alpha+\cos \beta=-\frac{27}{65}$, then the value of $\cos \frac{\alpha-\beta}{2}$ is
question: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be such that $\pi < \alpha-\beta < 3 \pi$. If $\sin \alpha+\sin \beta=-\frac{21}{65}$ and $\cos \alpha+\cos \beta=-\frac{27}{65}$, then the value of $\cos \frac{\alpha-\beta}{2}$ is
solution:
$\sin \alpha+\sin \beta=-\frac{21}{65}$ and $\cos \alpha+\cos \beta=-\frac{27}{65}$.
Squaring and adding, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2+2 \cos (\alpha-\beta)=\frac{1170}{(65)^2} \\
& \Rightarrow \cos ^2\left(\frac{\alpha-\beta}{2}\right)=\frac{9}{130} \Rightarrow \cos \left(\frac{\alpha-\beta}{2}\right)=\frac{-3}{\sqrt{130}} \quad\left(\because \frac{\pi}{2} < \frac{\alpha-\beta}{2} < \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right) .
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\sin x+\sin ^2 x=1, x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$, then $\left(\cos ^{12} x+\tan ^{12} x\right)+3\left(\cos ^{10} x+\tan ^{10} x+\cos ^8 x+\tan ^8 x\right)+\left(\cos ^6 x+\tan ^6 x\right)$ is equal to :
question: If $\sin x+\sin ^2 x=1, x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$, then $\left(\cos ^{12} x+\tan ^{12} x\right)+3\left(\cos ^{10} x+\tan ^{10} x+\cos ^8 x+\tan ^8 x\right)+\left(\cos ^6 x+\tan ^6 x\right)$ is equal to :
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \sin x+\sin ^2 x=1 \\
& \Rightarrow \sin x=\cos ^2 x \Rightarrow \tan x=\cos x
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Given expression
$\begin{aligned}
& =2 \cos ^{12} x+6\left[\cos ^{10} x+\cos ^8 x\right]+2 \cos ^6 x \\
& =2\left[\sin ^6 x+3 \sin ^5 x+3 \sin ^4 x+\sin ^3 x\right] \\
& =2 \sin ^3 x\left[(\sin x+1)^3\right] \\
& =2\left[\sin ^2 x+\sin x\right]^3 \\
& =2
\end{aligned}$

question: The value of  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>9</mn><msup><mrow/><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>27</mn><msup><mrow/><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>63</mn><msup><mrow/><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>81</mn><msup><mrow/><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup></math> is _____.
solution:
Given,

tan9o-tan27o-tan63o+tan81o

=cot81°+tan81o-tan27°+cot27o

=tan9°+cot9°-tan27°+cot27°

=sin9°cos9°+cos9°sin9°-sin27°cos27°+cos27°sin27°

=2sin18o-2sin54o

=2×45-1-2×45+1

=815-1-15+1

=85+1-5-15-15+1

=825-1=4

question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
sinθ+cosθ=12

sin2θ+cos2θ+2sinθcosθ=14⇒sin2θ=-34

Now:

cos4θ=1-2sin22θ

=1-2-342

=1-2×916=-18

And sin6θ=3sin2θ-4sin32θ

=3-4sin22θ·sin2θ

=3-4916·-34

=34×-34=-916

So, 16sin2θ+cos4θ+sin6θ

=16-34-18-916=-23

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{18}\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{18}\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{7\pi}{18}\right) = K$, then the value of $\sin\left(\dfrac{10K\pi}{3}\right)$ is :
question: If $\sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{18}\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{18}\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{7\pi}{18}\right) = K$, then the value of $\sin\left(\dfrac{10K\pi}{3}\right)$ is :
solution:
Given $K = \sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{18}\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{18}\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{7\pi}{18}\right)$

Using the standard trigonometric identity $\sin(\theta) \sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{3} - \theta\right) \sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{3} + \theta\right) = \dfrac{1}{4} \sin(3\theta)$ with $\theta = \dfrac{\pi}{18}$:

$\dfrac{\pi}{3} - \theta = \dfrac{6\pi}{18} - \dfrac{\pi}{18} = \dfrac{5\pi}{18}$

$\dfrac{\pi}{3} + \theta = \dfrac{6\pi}{18} + \dfrac{\pi}{18} = \dfrac{7\pi}{18}$

Applying the identity, we get:

$K = \dfrac{1}{4} \sin\left(3 \times \dfrac{\pi}{18}\right) = \dfrac{1}{4} \sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{6}\right)$

$K = \dfrac{1}{4} \times \dfrac{1}{2} = \dfrac{1}{8}$

We need to find the value of $\sin\left(\dfrac{10K\pi}{3}\right)$. Substituting $K = \dfrac{1}{8}$:

$\sin\left(\dfrac{10 \times \dfrac{1}{8} \times \pi}{3}\right) = \sin\left(\dfrac{10\pi}{24}\right) = \sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{12}\right)$

Expanding $\dfrac{5\pi}{12}$ as $\dfrac{\pi}{4} + \dfrac{\pi}{6}$:

$\sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{12}\right) = \sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{4} + \dfrac{\pi}{6}\right)$

$\sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{12}\right) = \sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{4}\right)\cos\left(\dfrac{\pi}{6}\right) + \cos\left(\dfrac{\pi}{4}\right)\sin\left(\dfrac{\pi}{6}\right)$

$\sin\left(\dfrac{5\pi}{12}\right) = \left(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\left(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) + \left(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right) = \dfrac{\sqrt{3} + 1}{2\sqrt{2}}$

Answer: $\dfrac{\sqrt{3} + 1}{2\sqrt{2}}$

If the area of the quadrilateral is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math> sq. units, then the perimeter of the quadrilateral is
question: The lengths of two adjacent sides of a cyclic quadrilateral are <math><mn>2</mn></math> units and <math><mn>5</mn></math> units and the angle between them is <math><msup><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>o</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the area of the quadrilateral is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math> sq. units, then the perimeter of the quadrilateral is
solution:
cos⁡ 60 o =4+25-c22.2.5

⇒10=29-c2

⇒c2=19

⇒c=19

Now,

cos120°= a 2 + b 2 − c 2 2ab

⇒ -12=a2+b2-192ab

⇒a2+b2-19=-ab

⇒a2+b2+ab=19

=12 units

q: The number of <math><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mi> </mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is:
question: The number of <math><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mi> </mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is:
solution:
Given

2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x- 2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x=1

⇒2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x- 2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x= ±1

⇒2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x= ±1+ 2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

By squaring both the sides, we get

2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x=1+2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 2sin4⁡x-cos4⁡x+18cos2⁡x-sin2⁡x=1±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 2sin2⁡x-cos2⁡x+18cos2⁡x-sin2⁡x=1±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 16cos2⁡x-sin2⁡x=1±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 16cos⁡2x-1=±221+cos⁡2x22+91-cos⁡2x

Squaring both sides again, we get

256cos2⁡2x+1-32cos⁡2x=41+2cos⁡2x+cos2⁡2x2+91-cos⁡2x

256cos2⁡2x+1-32cos⁡2x=219-16cos⁡2x+cos2⁡2x

⇒ 254cos2⁡2x=37⇒ cos2⁡2x=37254⇒ cos⁡2x=±37254∈-1, 1
Since, the solutions lie in all four quadrants, there are 8 solutions which satisfy the equation.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo></math> for some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><msup><mi>sec</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mo>cosec</mo><mn>6</mn></msup><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to :
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo></math> for some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><msup><mi>sec</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mo>cosec</mo><mn>6</mn></msup><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to :

=250

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\frac{\pi}{2} \leq x \leq \frac{3 \pi}{4}$, then $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x\right)$ is equal to
question: If $\frac{\pi}{2} \leq x \leq \frac{3 \pi}{4}$, then $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x\right)$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x \\ & \text { Let } \tan \alpha=\frac{5}{12}, \alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \sin \alpha=\frac{5}{13}, \cos \alpha=\frac{12}{13}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x=\cos \alpha \cos x+\sin \alpha \sin x \\ & \quad=\cos (x-\alpha) \\ & \Rightarrow \cos ^{-1}[\cos (x-\alpha)]=x-\alpha \\ & \quad=x-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{5}{12}\right)\end{aligned}$

q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>6</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>6</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given, cos2π7+cos4π7+cos6π7

=12sinπ72cos2π7sinπ7+2cos4π7sinπ7+2cos6π7sinπ7

=12sinπ7sin3π7-sinπ7+sin5π7-sin3π7+sin7π7-sin5π7

Since, 2cosAsinB=sinA+B-sinA-B

=12sinπ7-sinπ7+sinπ

=-12, ∵sinπ=0

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\frac{\cos ^{2} 48^{\circ}-\sin ^{2} 12^{\circ}}{\sin ^{2} 24^{\circ}-\sin ^{2} 6^{\circ}}=\frac{\alpha+\beta \sqrt{5}}{2}$, where $\alpha, \beta \in \mathbb{N}$, then $\alpha+\beta$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_\_$
question: If $\frac{\cos ^{2} 48^{\circ}-\sin ^{2} 12^{\circ}}{\sin ^{2} 24^{\circ}-\sin ^{2} 6^{\circ}}=\frac{\alpha+\beta \sqrt{5}}{2}$, where $\alpha, \beta \in \mathbb{N}$, then $\alpha+\beta$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_\_$
solution:
Using $\cos^2A - \sin^2B = \cos(A+B)\cos(A-B)$:
$\cos^2 48° - \sin^2 12° = \cos 60° \cos 36° = \frac{1}{2}\cos 36°$.
Using $\sin^2A - \sin^2B = \sin(A+B)\sin(A-B)$:
$\sin^2 24° - \sin^2 6° = \sin 30° \sin 18° = \frac{1}{2}\sin 18°$.
Ratio $= \frac{\cos 36°}{\sin 18°} = \frac{(1+\sqrt{5})/4}{(\sqrt{5}-1)/4} = \frac{(1+\sqrt{5})^2}{4} = \frac{6+2\sqrt{5}}{4} = \frac{3+\sqrt{5}}{2}$.
$\alpha = 3,\ \beta = 1$. $\alpha + \beta = 4$.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>cosec </mtext><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mtext>p</mtext><mo>+</mo><mtext>q</mtext></mrow><mrow><mtext>  p</mtext><mo>-</mo><mtext>q  </mtext></mrow></mfrac><mfenced><mrow><mtext>p</mtext><mo>≠</mo><mtext>q, p</mtext><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo></math>then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mtext>cot</mtext><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equals to:

solution:
cosec  θ = p + q p - q ⇒ 1 sin  θ = p + q p - q

let  tan θ 2 = t    then    1 + t 2 2t = p + q p - q  ∵sin2θ=2tanθ1+tan2θ

Using componendo and dividendo rule, we get

⇒ 1 + t 2 + 2t 1 + t 2 - 2t = p + q + p - q p + q - p + q  

⇒ 1 + t 1 - t 2 = p q

⇒  |1-t1+t|  =qp=|cot(π4+θ)| ∵tanπ4+θ=1+tanθ1-tanθ

question: If <math><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>13</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> and <math><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow></math> is equal to:

cosα+β=35⇒tanα+β=43

sin(α-β)=513⇒tan(α-β)=512

Now,

tan2α=tanα+β+α-β

            =tan(α+β)+tan(α-β)1-tan(α+β)tan(α-β)

            =43+5121-43×512=6316

subject: Mathematics
q: If $A = \dfrac{\sin 3^\circ}{\cos 9^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 9^\circ}{\cos 27^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 27^\circ}{\cos 81^\circ}$ and $B = \tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ$, then $\dfrac{B}{A}$ is equal to _____.
question: If $A = \dfrac{\sin 3^\circ}{\cos 9^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 9^\circ}{\cos 27^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 27^\circ}{\cos 81^\circ}$ and $B = \tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ$, then $\dfrac{B}{A}$ is equal to _____.
solution:
Consider the general term $\dfrac{\sin x}{\cos 3x}$.

We can rewrite this by multiplying and dividing by $2\cos x$:

$\dfrac{2\sin x \cos x}{2\cos 3x \cos x} = \dfrac{\sin 2x}{2\cos 3x \cos x}$

Using $\sin 2x = \sin(3x - x) = \sin 3x \cos x - \cos 3x \sin x$, we get:

$\dfrac{\sin 3x \cos x - \cos 3x \sin x}{2\cos 3x \cos x} = \dfrac{1}{2} \left( \dfrac{\sin 3x}{\cos 3x} - \dfrac{\sin x}{\cos x} \right) = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 3x - \tan x)$

Applying this identity to each term of $A$:

$\dfrac{\sin 3^\circ}{\cos 9^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 9^\circ - \tan 3^\circ)$

$\dfrac{\sin 9^\circ}{\cos 27^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 27^\circ - \tan 9^\circ)$

$\dfrac{\sin 27^\circ}{\cos 81^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 81^\circ - \tan 27^\circ)$

Adding these terms, the series telescopes:

$A = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 9^\circ - \tan 3^\circ + \tan 27^\circ - \tan 9^\circ + \tan 81^\circ - \tan 27^\circ)$

$A = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ)$

We are given $B = \tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ$.

Substituting $B$ into the equation for $A$:

$A = \dfrac{1}{2} B$

$\Rightarrow \dfrac{B}{A} = 2$

Answer: $2$

q: If $\sin x=-\frac{3}{5}$, where $\pi < x < \frac{3 \pi}{2}$, then $80\left(\tan ^2 x-\cos x\right)$ is equal to
question: If $\sin x=-\frac{3}{5}$, where $\pi < x < \frac{3 \pi}{2}$, then $80\left(\tan ^2 x-\cos x\right)$ is equal to
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sin x=\frac{-3}{5}, \pi < x < \frac{3 \pi}{2} \\ & \tan x=\frac{3}{4} \cos x=-\frac{4}{5} \\ & 80\left(\tan ^2 x-\cos x\right) \\ & =80\left(\frac{9}{16}+\frac{4}{5}\right)=45+64=109\end{aligned}$

q: If $\cot x=\frac{5}{12}$ for some $x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)$, then $\sin 7 x\left(\cos \frac{13 x}{2}+\sin \frac{13 x}{2}\right)+\cos 7 x\left(\cos \frac{13 x}{2}-\sin \frac{13 x}{2}\right)$ is equal to
question: If $\cot x=\frac{5}{12}$ for some $x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)$, then $\sin 7 x\left(\cos \frac{13 x}{2}+\sin \frac{13 x}{2}\right)+\cos 7 x\left(\cos \frac{13 x}{2}-\sin \frac{13 x}{2}\right)$ is equal to
solution:
$\cot x = \dfrac{5}{12} \Rightarrow \cos x = \dfrac{-5}{13} = 2\cos^2 \dfrac{x}{2} - 1$
$\cos\left(\dfrac{x}{2}\right) = -\dfrac{2}{\sqrt{13}}$ or $\dfrac{2}{\sqrt{13}}$ (rejected)
$\left\{ \because \dfrac{x}{2} \in \left(\dfrac{\pi}{2}, \dfrac{3\pi}{4}\right) \right\}$
$\left(\sin 7x \dfrac{\sin 13x}{2} + \cos 7x \dfrac{\cos 13x}{2}\right) + \left(\sin 7x \dfrac{\cos 13x}{2} - \cos 7x \dfrac{\sin 13x}{2}\right)$
$\cos\left(7x - \dfrac{13x}{2}\right) + \sin\left(7x - \dfrac{13x}{2}\right)$
$\cos \dfrac{x}{2} + \sin\left(\dfrac{x}{2}\right)$
$\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{13}} - \dfrac{2}{\sqrt{13}} = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{13}}$

=96×132=3

tan⁡α+2β=tanα+tan2β1-tanαtan⁡2β=17+341-17·34=4+21282528=1
